MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HAMILTON MATERIALS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
RULING: HEARING CONTINUED TO AUGUST 22, 2005 TO ALLOW COMPLETION OF JAMES ROBLES DEPOSITION PRIOR TO HEARING. (302/EHG/JT). Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489

Motion For Protective Order Deeming Depos Of James Robles Concluded & Request For Sanctions; Points & Authorities; Declaration
Off Calendar Aug-08-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff for defendant Certainteed Corporation
Anna Pinski for defendant Crane Co.

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for a protective order and to deem the deposition of James Robles completed:
Granted in part and denied in part. The motion to deem the deposition of James Robles completed is denied. The motion for a protective order is granted in part. The deposition is to resume with defendants allocated an additional eight hours on the record to complete their examination. While the Court finds that several defense counsel unnecessarily prolonged the examination and engaged in redundant questioning, counsel for plaintiff on numerous occasions interposed objections that lacked merit and impeded the deposition by lengthy statements for the record. The deposition is to resume at a time mutually convenient to the parties and is to be completed by August 19, 2005. All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
